mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 8.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive delta condition yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO deltas fail to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index exceeding 225.0 indicates accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods following CPIAUCSL crossing 225.0, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer confidence, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined positive payroll and negative unemployment delta condition produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from noise (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, creating immediate positive price pressure on the S&P 500 over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates correspond to negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines greater than 1.0% are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly (t-test p > 0.10).
